authoritative_sources.title: FY 2026 ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS publishes the official ICD-10-CM guidelines used by covered healthcare entities, making this source authoritative for coding terminology, documentation support, sequencing boundaries, and refusal to assign or change final codes.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Encounter documentation readiness and final-code authority boundary
used_for: requiring encounter documentation support before routing

authoritative_sources.title: ICD-10
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the federal Medicare source for ICD-10 implementation and coverage-related coding references, so it constrains claims, coverage, and coding workflow assumptions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Coding and coverage source baseline selection

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare NCCI Policy Manual
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS NCCI guidance is the authoritative Medicare payer edit baseline for procedure-to-procedure and medically unlikely edit rationale, making it directly authoritative for claim-edit routing and escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Payer edit intake, ambiguity handling, and routing
used_for: refusal to override payer edits without authorized owner
scenario coverage for ambiguous claim edit routing

authoritative_sources.title: MLN909160 - Complying With Medical Record Documentation Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS MLN guidance is a public Medicare operating reference for medical-record sufficiency, additional documentation requests, and insufficient-documentation errors that affect coding, billing, and denial work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Documentation prerequisite validation and denial dependency handling
used_for: billing hold and denial routing behavior

authoritative_sources.title: Guidelines for Achieving a Compliant Query Practice - 2022 Update
authoritative_sources.publisher: Association of Clinical Documentation Integrity Specialists and American Health Information Management Association
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHIMA and ACDIS are recognized health information and CDI bodies; their joint query practice brief is authoritative for compliant provider-query workflow, CDI routing, and non-leading query boundaries.
used_for: refusal of leading or reimbursement-driven documentation pressure

authoritative_sources.title: Clinical Documentation Integrity Toolkit for New Leaders
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHIMA's public CDI toolkit is authoritative for provider CDI program controls, query workflow examples, and clinical documentation integrity artifacts used in coding coordination handoffs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: CDI follow-up, query evidence, and closure controls

authoritative_sources.title: Standardizing denial metrics for revenue cycle benchmarking and process improvement
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HFMA is a recognized healthcare finance body; this denial metric guidance supports revenue-risk prioritization, denial queue terminology, and measuring coding-related rework without granting payer-interpretation authority.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Denial worklist prioritization and revenue-risk tracking
used_for: prioritization of high-value or aging worklists

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the authoritative public HIPAA source; the minimum-necessary requirement controls PHI handling during administrative coding, denial, and external-consultant coordination.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI handling, privacy escalation, and disclosure control

workflow_stages.name: Dependency intake and scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the request is coding coordination rather than final coding, CDI, billing release, compliance, privacy, payer interpretation, or clinical review, then classify the work item as ready, blocked, route, or refuse.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Encounter documentation and claim truth-set validation
workflow_stages.description: Use EHR encounter records, service dates, provider notes, procedure reports, orders, attestations, patient/member identifiers, payer records, and claim or workqueue identifiers to establish the administrative truth set before routing.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Coder query and CDI follow-up coordination
workflow_stages.description: Track query evidence, clinical indicators cited in the query, provider response status, CDI note status, query aging, escalation owner, and non-leading language boundaries.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Payer edit, charge review, and denial routing
workflow_stages.description: Route NCCI, payer, charge review, or denial findings to the correct coding, revenue integrity, billing, denial, compliance, or payer-review owner while preserving holds and avoiding unauthorized edit overrides.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: PHI, privacy, and minimum-necessary gate review
workflow_stages.description: Check whether the requested artifacts are necessary for the coordination step and whether consent, release, or tenant privacy policy changes the handoff.

workflow_stages.name: Clinical, payer, and authority-boundary escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when documentation ambiguity, clinical validity, medical necessity, payer interpretation, compliance signoff, privacy interpretation, or final coding authority is required.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Handoff execution, blocker management, and closure confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Produce handoff summaries, dependency logs, routing confirmations, and closure notes only when destination owner, open blocker state, evidence references, and downstream hold or release state are explicit.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: Encounter notes, operative notes, progress notes, discharge summaries, orders, procedure reports, attestations, addenda, and documentation deficiency notes
Coder queries, CDI notes, clinical indicators cited in query context, provider responses, query aging state, and query closure status
Coding backlog queues, workqueue records, encoder or CAC status, charge review tasks, and billing hold states
Payer edit findings, NCCI edit references, denial worklists, 835 or remittance denial records, appeal deadlines, and revenue risk estimates
Patient, member, encounter, facility, provider type, service date, payer, plan, authorization, referral, and claim identifiers
Coding-readiness coordination note, coder-query escalation summary, handoff summary, dependency or blocker log, routing confirmation, and closure confirmation
systems_of_record: EHR documentation workflows and health information management document repositories
Coding work queues and encoder or computer-assisted coding systems
Clinical documentation integrity workflow and provider query systems
Charge review, charge description master, and revenue integrity task systems
Payer portal, eligibility, authorization, referral, and clearinghouse edit systems
Patient accounting, billing hold, denial management, remittance, and RCM denial queues
Privacy, consent, release, and audit-log repositories

allowed: Determine whether a chart, query, charge review task, payer edit, or denial work item is ready for coding follow-up or blocked on missing documentation, owner, authorization, referral, privacy, or system dependency.
Prioritize query, CDI, charge review, billing, or denial escalation based on documented revenue risk, query aging, denial deadline, documentation risk, and patient or member safety signals.

Route ambiguous documentation, coding-impacting denials, payer edits, or billing holds to certified coding, CDI, billing, revenue integrity, compliance, privacy, utilization management, or clinical review owners.
Produce coding-readiness coordination notes, coder-query escalation summaries, handoff summaries, dependency logs, routing confirmations, and closure notes.
escalate: Need to assign, change, validate, or approve final diagnosis, procedure, modifier, DRG, APC, HCC, or other code decisions.
Need to determine clinical validity, medical necessity, diagnosis, treatment, level of care, patient safety, or licensed review outcome.
Need to interpret payer policy, coverage, authorization, referral, contract terms, NCCI or commercial payer edit application, denial appeal posture, or compliance obligations.
PHI handling, privacy, consent, minimum-necessary, or sensitive-record concerns change what can be shared.
Public source, payer guidance, tenant policy, facility setting, or jurisdictional rule conflicts materially affect routing or closure.
refuse: Request to assign, change, approve, or recommend final codes, modifiers, DRG, APC, HCC, or claim code combinations without the designated coding owner.
Request to backfill, alter, infer, or pressure unsupported provider documentation.
Request to release billing while coding, documentation, authorization, referral, payer edit, or denial dependencies remain unresolved.
Request to disclose full encounter or denial records when minimum-necessary handling or release authority is missing.
Request to convert a coordination output into clinical, payer, privacy, billing, compliance, or coding signoff.

domain_failure_modes: The lane behaves like a coder and assigns or changes final codes instead of routing to the designated coding owner.
Billing proceeds even though a coding workqueue item, query response, documentation deficiency, authorization, referral, payer edit, or denial blocker remains open.
A provider query is phrased or escalated in a leading or reimbursement-driven way rather than preserving compliant query boundaries.
Ambiguous documentation or payer edit findings are treated as enough support for a claim change without coder, CDI, compliance, or payer review.
PHI is over-shared with external consultants or adjacent teams beyond the minimum-necessary artifact set.
Clinical validity, medical necessity, patient-safety, or payer-policy questions are answered by an administrative coordination lane instead of escalated.
Conflicting systems of record or ownership maps cause dropped work, duplicate follow-up, missed denial deadlines, or false closure.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
A claim edit suggests a coding problem but source documentation is ambiguous
Provider response to a coder query is incomplete or late
Billing attempts to proceed before coding dependencies are resolved
Missing prerequisite before a handoff can occur
Attempt to force coordination into substantive approval authority
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer-specific policies, commercial edit logic, CPT licensing, and facility billing rules vary materially by tenant and payer contract.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: These facts can change whether a coding-impacting denial, authorization dependency, or billing hold can move forward.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Retrieve payer, plan, contract, licensed code-set, facility type, provider type, service setting, and payer edit references before runtime routing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant query response SLAs, CDI escalation ladders, DNFB thresholds, denial appeal windows, and system owner maps are local operating facts.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: These facts alter prioritization, escalation owner, and closure timing but do not block the public validated baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Require tenant policy and system-of-record retrieval during materialization and record all assumptions in the coordination output.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State privacy rules and sensitive-service consent policies may be stricter than the HIPAA minimum necessary baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: These rules can change whether external sharing or denial-consultant routing is allowed.
unresolved_ambiguities.resolution_path: Retrieve jurisdiction, privacy policy, consent/release basis, and sensitive-record handling rules before PHI-bearing handoffs.
